ety: inf. n. vi of قعد 'to sit'
def: s.m. Backwardness, dilatoriness, negligence.
s.m. = ṭakā, q.v. s.m. A ready, pert, or plain answer; a point-blank refusal
subs: s.f. A sole individual life without relations or dependants

ety: from A. تقي
def: s.f. Piety; abstinence, continence (see taqwā).
more: (from تقي or وقي) s. f. Piety, abstinence, continence.

ety: inf. n. vi of قوي 'to be strong'
def: s.f. Strengthening, assisting (particularly tenants); money in advance (esp. to cultivators for implements, digging wells, &c.); — vying, contending (with).
more: n. f. Pecuniary advance, especially to cultivators for implements or digging wells, etc.
(inf. vi. of قوي) s. f. 1. Strengthening, assisting (particularly, tenants). 2. Vying, contending with. 3. Money in advance.

ety: inf. n. v of قوي 'to be strong'
def: s.f. Strengthening, assisting (particularly tenants, by advances, remitting rents, &c.; cf. taqāwī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْویٰ (taqwā), borrowed from Arabic تَقْوَى (taqwā).

ety: for A. taqāẓī, inf. n. vi of قضي 'to decree; to pay (a debt),' &c.; — in A. nouns of this form the Persians generally change final ī to ā, as tamāshā for tamāshī, &c.
def: s.m. Demanding or exacting payment (of a debt), dunning; pressing the settlement of a claim; demand, requisition, claim; exigence, urgency, importunity
more: (for تقاضي inf. vi. of قضي) s. m. Dunning, exacting, exigence, urgency, importunity. تقاضاي سن taḳāẓā-i-sinn, The customs or habits of different ages, as of childhood, youth, manhood, &c. تقاضاي شديد taḳāẓā-i-shadīd, Inexorable dunning.
subs: s.m. The customs or habits of different ages (as of childhood, youth, or manhood); the requirements, or the natural tendencies, of different ages
s.m. Great pressure or importunity; inexorable dunning
(-kā), To demand payment (of); to exact; to claim; to press, to urge, to dun

def: To claim.
adj. Exacting, pressing, urging, importunate, solicitous.
adj. Importunate, urging, solicitous.
s. f. Nick of time.

ety: inf. n. ii of کبر
def: s.f. lit. 'Making, calling, or esteeming great'; magnifying God (by saying allāhǒ akbar, 'God is great'); repeating the Mohammadan creed, or only saying 'God is great' upon particular occasions.
more: n. f. 1. The praises of God. 2. Repetition of the Mah. creed ( kalmā , q. v). Hāzir ko luqmā, gāyab ko takbīr . Prov. A mouthful for all present, a blessing to the absent.
(inf. ii. of کبر) s. f. Repeating the Musalmān creed; or only saying, "God is great," upon particular occasions.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic تَكْبِير (takbīr).

ety: dim. of t̤āq, q.v.
def: s.m. A small recess, a niche; a small shelf; a small window, &c.
s. m. dimin. of طاق q. v.

ety: inf. n. ii of قدم 'to precede'
def: s.f. Giving precedence (to), preferring, putting before; precedence, priority; sending forward, sending on before; setting before, presenting; preparing; performing; putting one before another, transposition
more: (inf. ii. of قدم) s. f. Giving precedence, priority, precedence, performance. تقديم کرنا taḳdīm karnā, To perform, to put before, to give precedence to.
subs: v.t. To give precedence (to); to send forward, &c.; to put before; to prepare, to perform.

ety: for A. تقدمة taqdimat, fem., inf. n. ii of قدم
def: s.m. The proposition (of a syllogism); advance (of money on account); final adjustment or audit (of an account); an estimate; a budget.
more: n. m. Final adjustment or audit of an account; an estimate ( takhmīnā ); a budget.

ety: inf. n. ii of قدر 'to measure, determine,' &c.
def: s.f. Measuring; apportioning (subsistence or daily bread); ordering, ordaining, decreeing; — thinking, considering, supposing; the ordaining of Providence; the Divine decree; predestination; fate, destiny, lot
more: n. f. A. قدر measuring. Fate (پراليدهـ). 1. Āyā merī taqdīr kā likkhā mere āge . Rahīm. What was written in my fate has come before me. 2. Taqdīr sīdhi hai to sab kuchh . Prov. Saying. Your fate propitious all goes right. 3. Sārā khel taqdīr kā hai . Prov. Saying. All things are the sport of fate. taqdīr kā heṭā, karam-hīn , n. m. An unfortunate or unlucky man. See abhāgā .
(inf. ii. of قدر) s. f. Fate, destiny, divine decree, predestination, lot. (Plur. تقديرات).
subs: adj. Having a wretched lot; — s.m. A wretched wight
adv. On the supposition; in the event (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْدِیر (taqdīr), from Arabic تَقْدِير (taqdīr).

def: s.f. pl. (of taqdīr), Decrees, &c. (of Providence).

ety: inf. n. ii of قدس 'to be pure or holy'
def: s.f. Making pure or holy, sanctifying; sanctification, sanctity; purity; magnifying.
more: (inf. ii. of قدس) s. f. Sanctification, sanctity, purity, sanctifying; magnifying.

ety: inf. n. ii of کفن 'to wrap (a body) in a winding-sheet'
def: s.f. Putting on the winding-sheet, laying in the coffin, shrouding; sepulture
more: (inf. ii. of کفن) s. f. Putting on the winding sheet, laying in the coffin, &c. shrouding, sepulture. تکفين کرنا takfīn karna, To shroud, to coffin.

ety: inf. n. ii of کفر 'to cover, to hide; to be ungrateful,' &c.
def: s.f. Covering; expiating a crime, doing penance or paying a mulct as atonement (for); humbling oneself before another (putting the hand upon the breast and inclining the head), shewing distant respect; — accusing (one) of infidelity or impiety, calling (one) an infidel; making (one) an unbeliever; guiltiness.
more: n. f. See کفر 1. Expiating a crime; doing penance or paying a mulct as atonement. 2. Accusing one of impiety; denouncing as an infidel.

(inf. ii. of کفر or کفاره) s. f. 1. Covering. 2. Expiating a crime, doing penance or paying a mulct as atonement. 3. Humbling oneʼs self before another (putting the hand upon the breast, and inclining the head), showing distant respect. 4. Accusing one of impiety, calling him infidel, making one an unbeliever. 5. Guiltiness.

ety: akin to Zend thwakhsh; S. tvaksh; cf. taḵẖt
def: s.m. Fire-wood; a veil; darkness.
more: s. m. 1. A veil. 2. Firewood. 3. Darkness.

ety: inf. n. v of خيل 'to think; to fancy'
def: s.m. Imagining, fancying, supposing; imagination, fancy ( = ḵẖayāl); suspicion.
more: (inf. v. of خيال) s. m. 1. Fancying, imagining, supposing. 2. Suspicion. Plur. تخيلات taḵẖaiyulāt, 1. Suspicions. 2. Fancies.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَخَیُّل (taxayyul), from Arabic تَخَيُّل (taḵayyul).

def: s.m. pl. (of taḵẖaiyul), Imaginations, fancies; suspicions.

ety: inf. n. v of خيم 'to raise'
def: s.m. Pitching a tent or tents.
more: (inf. v. of خيم) s. Pitching of a tent or tents.

ety: inf. n. ii of خلخل
def: s.m. (?) Attiring oneself with, or putting on, an anklet (ḵẖalḵẖāl); — coming to pieces, becoming old and worn out (a garment); separation.
more: (inf. ii. of خلخل) s. 1. Putting on or wearing the ornament called خلخال ḵẖalḵẖāl. 2. Separation.

ety: inf. n. v of خلف 'to follow'
def: s.m. Remaining behind, being left behind; delaying; going back.
more: (inf. v. of خلف) s. m. Delaying, going back, remaining or being left behind.

ety: inf. n. v of خلّ 'to perforate,' &c.
def: s.m. Disturbance, discord, dissension; interruption; perforation.
more: (inf. v. of خلل) s. m. Disturbance, interruption, discord, dissension.

ety: inf. n. v of خلص 'to become safe or secure'
def: s.m. The nom de plame assumed by poets (as Saudā was the taḵẖalluṣ of Mirza Mohammad Rafīʻ).
more: n. m. خلص he chose it The nom de plume assumed by Persian, Arabic and Urdu poets.
(inf. v. of خلص) s. m. The titular name assumed by poets; as, Saudā was the taḵẖallūṣ of Mirzā Muhammad Rafīﻌ.

ety: inf. n. vi of خلف 'to turn from,' &c.
def: s.m. Mutual opposition or contention; antagonism, enmity.
more: (inf. vi. of خلف) s. m. 1. Opposition, contention. 2. Enmity.

def: (inf. vi. of خلص) s. (dakh.) Liberation.

ety: Prk. तक्खाणो; S. तक्षन्
def: s.m. A carpenter.
wety: PIE word *tétḱō Inherited from Sanskrit तक्षन् (takṣan, “carpenter; woodcutter”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *táṭṣā, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *táćšā, from Proto-Indo-European *tétḱō (“carpenter, woodcutter”).

def: s.m. = takshaṇ and takshan, qq.v. s.m. Cutting, paring, peeling, planing.

ety: S. तत्+क्षण
def: adv. (prov.) At that time, then (correl. of jakhan).

ety: tak, q.v.+Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Scales (of a balance); a pair of small scales.

ety: tak, q.v.+Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Scales (of a balance); a pair of small scales.

ety: inf. n. vi of خرج 'to come out,' &c.
def: s.m. Taking a property by mutual consent and dividing it (the co-inheritors, — one taking the house, another the land, and so on; or by selling the property and then dividing the sum realized; or by one's taking ready money and the other's taking a debt).

ety: inf. n. ii of خفّ 'to be light'
def: s.f. Making light, alleviating; abbreviating; making (a word) easy of utterance, softening the pronunciation (of letters) by changing one for another, euphoniæ gratia; holding or esteeming light, despising; alleviation, abatement, mitigation, remission, relief, palliation, extenuation; abatement, diminution, decrease; reduction; decay
more: n. f. خف it became less. 1. Diminution; abatement. 2. Reduction; retrenchment; saving. 3. Remission; mitigation; relief. takhfīf k . v. a. 1. To lessen; abate; lower. 2. To mitigate; relax; remit; relieve. 3. To retrench; reduce; make a saving. takhfīf meṅ lānā , v. a. To retrench; abolish; effect a saving.

(inf. ii. of خّف) s. f. 1. Making light, alleviating. 2. Despising. 3. Abbreviating. 4. Alleviation, abatement, relief, remission, abridgment, mitigation, palliation, extenuation, decrease, diminution, decay. 5. Softening the pronunciation of letters, or changing one for another euphoniæ gratia. تخفيف تصديع taḵẖfīf-i-taṣdīﻌ, 1. Banishing care. 2. Relieving pain or trouble. تخفيف دينا taḵẖfīf-denā, To alleviate, assuage, &c. تخفيف کرنا taḵẖfīf karnā, To extenuate, mitigate, allay, remit.
subs: (-ko), To give or afford relief (to); to alleviate, mitigate, assuage, &c. (see next)
(-kī), To lighten, alleviate, mitigate, extenuate, relieve; to remit, relax; to reduce, diminish, lessen, abate
v.n. To come under reduction, to be reduced (an establishment or expenditure); to be abolished (an office or post)
v.t. To effect a reduction of (establishment, &c.), to reduce, to retrench; to abolish, do away with.

ety: inf. n. ii of اخر
def: s.f. Delay, procrastination; postponement; impediment
more: n. f. اخر going back. Delay; procrastination. tā/?/khīr k . v. a. To defer; retard; postpone ( āj kal k .).
(inf. ii. of اخرت) s. f. Delay, procrastination, impediment. تاخيرکرنا tāḵẖīr karnā, To delay, procrastinate, postpone, retard.
subs: (-meṅ), To delay, procrastinate; to postpone; to retard.

ety: inf. n. ii of خلص 'to become safe'
def: s.f. Saving, delivering; release; freedom.

ety: for A. تخلية taḵẖliyat, inf. n. ii of خلو 'to be empty'
def: s.f. Evacuation; private place or room, privacy; manumission (of a slave); divorce (of a wife).
more: n. m. خلا was or became empty. 1. Evacuation. 2. Privacy; a private room.
(inf. ii. of خلوت) s. m. 1. Evacuation. 2. Privacy. 3. Manumission (of a slave). 4. Divorce (of a wife).

ety: from A. taḵẖmīn, inf. n. ii of خمن 'to be unapparent'
def: s.m. Surmise, guess, conjecture; appraisement, valuation, estimate (syn. andāza)
more: n. m. خمن he surmised. Estimate. See اندازه 1. takhmīnā karnā , v. n. To estimate. pakkā ta/?/khmīnah , n. m. Precise estimate. kachchā ta/?/khmīnah , n. m. Rough estimate. māliat-i-ta/?/khmīnah , Estimated value.
(inf. ii. of خمن) Guess, appraisement, conjecture, valuation.
subs: (-kā), To make a conjecture (concerning); to judge (of); to value, appraise, estimate.

ety: acc. of taḵẖmīna, q.v.
def: adv. At a guess, by guess, conjecturally; by appraisement; hypothetically; — nearly, about, more or less.
more: adv. 1. By appraisement; on a rough estimate; on the whole. 2. More or less; about.
(from خمن) adv. 1. By guess, by conjecture, by appraisement. 2. Nearly, about.

ety: inf. n. ii of خمر 'to leaven'
def: s.f. Fermenting, forming into leaven; fermentation.
more: (inf. ii. of خمر) s. f. Fermentation, forming into leaven.

def: s.m. (local) = ṭaḵẖna, for ṭaknā, q.v.
s.m. The ankle = ṭakhnā, q.v.

ety: S. टङ्क+र or ल+कः
def: s.m. The ankle-joint, the ankle ( = ṭaknā; ṭakhnā).
more: (prop. ٿکنا) s. m. The ankle joint.

def: s.m. = ṭaḵẖnā, ṭaknā, qq.v.
s. m. (dakh.) Ankle ( Harris ).

ety: inf. n. ii of خرب 'to be in a state of ruin'
def: s.f. Reducing to ruin, demolishing, razing; demolition, devastation, destruction.
more: (inf. ii. of خرب Heb. Was desolate) s. f. Devastation, destruction, razing.

def: s.f. Making peculiar and special; reserving for oneself; appropriation; particularity, peculiarity, speciality.
more: n. f. خص he distinguished it. Peculiarity; particularity.
(inf. ii. of خّص) s. f. Particularity, peculiarity, appropriation, reserving for oneʼs self.

ety: Pehl. tākht; Zend rt. tac; S. rt. tañć
def: s.m. Assault, attack; invasion, incursion, inroad
more: n. f. P. tā/?/khtan to attack. Assault; attack; incursion; invasion.
(from تاختن) s. f. Assault, attack, incursion, inroad, invasion. تاخت کرنا tāḵẖt-karnā, To assault, attack, invade, ravage.
subs: tāḵẖt-o-tārāj, s.m. Assault and plunder, depredation, devastation, havoc, ravage
(-par), To assault, attack, invade; to lay waste, ravage
(-par), To lead an attack (upon), to attack, invade, &c.

ety: Zend thwakhshta, rt. thwakhsh; S. त्वष्ट, rt. त्वक्ष, 'to hew, fashion;' cf. taḵẖta
def: s.m. Throne, chair of state; seat, stage, platform; sofa, bed; any place raised above the ground for sitting, reclining, or sleeping
more: n. m. 1. A throne; a chair of state. Takht yā takhtah . Prov. A throne or the grave. (Victory or death). 2. A low table or seat. tahkt par biṭhānā , v. a. To seat on the throne. takht par baiṭhnā , v. n. To ascend or sit on the throne. takht-posh . A cloth or cushion to sit on. takht chhoṛnā , v. a. To abdicate a throne. takht-i-ravāṅ , n. 1. A travelling throne erected on a platform and carried on men's shoulders; a throne on which a king is carried. 2. A litter, especially for females or persons of rank. takht se utār'nā , v. a. To dethrone. takht kī rāt , Mah.; suhāg rāt , Hin., n. f. The bridal night; consummation of a marriage. takht-gāh , n. f. 1. The royal residence. 2. The capital; metropolis; seat of government. takht-nashīnī , n. f. Accession to the throne.

s. m. A throne. تخت بخت taḵẖt baḵẖt, 1. Throne and fortune. 2. Used as a form of benediction, particularly by women, to signify wedlock and wealth. تخت پربيٿهالنا taḵẖt par baiṭhālnā, To enthrone. تخت پربيٿهنا taḵẖt par baiṭhnā, To reign. تخت چهوڙنا taḵẖt chhoṛnā, To abdicate a throne. تخت دار taḵẖt-dār, s. m. A king. تخت روان taḵẖt-i-rawān, s. m. A travelling throne, a throne on which the king is carried. تخت سي اتارنا taḵẖt se utārnā, To dethrone. تخت نشان taḵẖt-nishān, Seating on, or bestowing thrones. تخت نشين taḵẖt-nishīn, Sitting on a throne, reigning; a king, a sovereign. تخت نشيني taḵẖt-nishīn-ī, s. f. Accession to the throne, reign. تخت يا تخته taḵẖt yā taḵẖta, Either a throne or a bier (spoken on engaging in a desperate enterprise).
subs: s.m. Throne and fortune; marriage-bed and offspring, wedlock and wealth (a form of benediction, particularly used by women)
v.t. To seat on a throne, to enthrone
v.n. To sit on a throne; to ascend the throne, to reign
s.m. A covering for a throne, seat, &c.; a cloth or cushion to sit on; a covered stage or platform
s.m. Possessor of a throne, a king
s.m. A travelling throne erected on a platform carried on men's shoulders, a throne on which the king is carried; the throne of Solomon; a litter with poles (esp. for persons of rank and females)
s.f. The bridal night; consummation of a marriage (syn. suhāg-rāt)
s.f. The royal residence; seat of government, metropolis, capital
adj. Seating on a throne, throne-bestowing
adj. & s.m. Sitting on a throne, reigning; a king, sovereign
s.f. Accession to the throne; reign
prov. A throne or a bier victory or death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تخت (taxt).

def: s. m. A game: see چوسر chausar.

ety: see taḵẖt
def: s.m. A plank, board; platform; deck (of a ship); drawbridge; table; bench; stool; pedestal (of a statue); sign-board; notice-board; a bier; a bed (of flowers, &c.), garden-plot; a sheet of paper
more: n. m. 1. A plank; board. 2. A bench; form. 3. A platform; deck; a gallows-board. 4. A signboard; notice board. 5. A drawbridge. 6. (Panj.) A bier. 7. A sheet of paper. 8. A flower bed; plot. takhtah-nard, takhta-i-nard , n. m. The game of chausar .

s. m. 1. A plank, a board; deck (of a ship). 2. A stool, bench. 3. A bier. 4. A sheet of paper. 5. A bed of flowers, &c. تخته بندي taḵẖta-bandī, s. f. Wainscot, boarding. تخته بندي کرنا taḵẖta-bandī karnā, To board, to plank, floor with boards. تخته گردن taḵẖta-gardan, adj. Thick, broad and straight necked (a horse). تخته نرد taḵẖta-nard, s. m. Backgammon.
subs: s.f. Wainscot, boarding; — taḵẖta-bandī karnā (-kī), To board, plank, to floor with boards
adj. Thick, broad, and straight-necked (a horse)
taḵẖta-ě-nard, s.m. The game of ćausar, q.v.; backgammon.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تخته (taxta), from Middle Persian tʾht' (taxt, “throne”) + -k' (-ag).

def: s. f. The royal residence, metropolis, capital.

ety: from taḵẖt
def: adj. Of a throne; becoming a throne; — ornamented with gold and silver leaf.

ety: dim. of taḵẖta
def: s.f. A small plank; a small board (like a slate) for children to write on; a tablet, a signet of stone; a leaf or thin plate of stained glass; agate, topaz, or other precious stone with a sentence of the Qorʼān inscribed or engraven on it worn hung from the neck of children and others (rather as an amulet than as an ornament); a plate (on which anything is engraved = paṭṭa); the breast.
more: n. f. Dim. of تخته 1. A small plank. 2. A small board used as a slate for beginners to write on. 3. An alphabet lesson. 4. A tablet; a signet of stone. 5. A leaf or thin plate of stained glass, agate, or other precious stone with a sentence of the Koran inscribed or engraved on it, hung from the necks of children and others rather as an amulet than an ornament. 6. The breast; chest. Takhtī terī agar bhalī hai, Tau sāṅche meṅ chhab merī ḍhalī hai . Wom. Rangīn. kandā kī-huī dhāt kī takhtī , n. f. An engraved plate.
(dimin. of تخته) s. f. 1. A small plank. 2. A little board like a slate, which children write on. 3. A signet of stone. 4. The breast.
adj. Ornamented with gold and silver leaf ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: A travelling throne erected on a platform carried on menʼs shoulders.
s. m. Assault and plunder, depredation, devastation. تاخت وتاراج کرنا tāḵẖt o tārāj karnā, v. a. To ruin, to depopulate.

ety: inf. n. ii of خوف 'to fear'
def: s.f. Putting in fear, terrifying; threatening; threat.
more: (inf. ii. of خوف) s. f. 1. Terrifying. 2. Threatening, threat.

def: s.f. A cheap trull ( = ṭakāʼī, q.v.). s.f. A tax, duty, collection, imposition; a cheap trull ( = ṭake-wālī).
adj. God-fearing, pious, devout.
more: adj. Pious, fearing God. ¶ tuḳạ̄, s. Piety, fear of God.

def: s.f. = tāk; ṭak, ṭakṭakī, q.v. s.f. Look, regard, glance, gaze; fixed look, stare; peep, watch, look-out, expectation; view, aim, the act of aiming (at); pointing (of a dog, &c.)
more: s. f. 1. Stare, fixed look. 2. Aim.
subs: s.f. Mutual gazing or staring, exchange of glances; bo-peep
s.f. Precise moment, nick of time
(-par), To fix the gaze (on), look fixedly (at); to take aim (at); to point (as a dog, &c.); to look longingly (at), to covet
(-kī), To come in expectation (of), to come with an eye (to)
tāk-meṅ rahnā (-kī), To be on the look out or watch (for), to lie in wait (for).

def: 1. adj. m. and f. Defective (horse), having the eyes of two different colours. 2. s. f. A kind of hat.

ety: P. dāg̠ī, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Defective (horse), having the eyes of two different colours; — a horse with such eyes; — s.f. A kind of hat.
conj.; see s.v. tā. s.m. A tail, the breast; the womb; the hip or flank; a warrior; a thief; a wicked man; an outcast, a barbarian; a jewel; nectar; a tree; — passing, crossing.
more: conj. In order that; in order to; to; with a view to; so that; to the end that.

ety: rel. n. fr. t̤āq, q.v.
def: adj. & s.m. Arched; vaulted; wall-eyed (a horse); squint-eyed (a inan); — a wall-eyed horse; a squint-eyed man; — s.f. Froth of urine.
more: adj. 1. Squint-eyed (a man). 2. Wall-eyed (a horse). tāqī , n. m. 1. A squint-eyed person. 2. A wall-eyed horse. Tāqī na rakkhe bāqī . Prov. In the house a squint-eyed man (or a wall-eyed horse) works all the ruin he can.
1. adj. Wall-eyed (a horse). 2. Arched. 3. s. f. Froth of urine.

ety: inf. n. ii of اکد for وکد 'to correspond (to),' &c.
def: s.f. Confirmation, ascertaining, strengthening, corroboration; stress, emphasis; injunction, order; reminder; pressure, compulsion; strict or close superintendence
more: n. f. اکد he trod wheat. 1. An injunction; order. 2. A reminder. 3. Emphasis. 4. Strictness; compulsion; force. 5. An order from a superior to an inferior officer strictly enjoining performance of any work or the execution of an order. tākīd karnā , v. a. To enjoin strictly; insist on; urge; press; caution. tākīd karvānā , v. a. To draw attention to; to have the attention drawn to. bahut tākīd ; Ped. tākīd-i-mazīd . Strict injunction.
(inf. ii. of اکد) s. f. 1. Confirmation, emphasis. 2. Injunction, ascertaining. 3. Superintendence. تاکيدکرنا tākīd karnā, To enjoin strictly, to urge, to press, insist.
subs: v.t. To enjoin strictly, to urge, press, insist on; to ascertain; to caution
v.t. To cause attention to be drawn to; to draw attention to (an order, &c.).

ety: inf. n. ii of عقد 'to tie,' &c.
def: s.f. Tying fast or strongly, knitting together, tying in knots; causing to unite, joining.
adv. Strictly; urgently; emphatically; positively; peremptorily.

ety: acc. of tākīd
def: adv. By way of confirmation or corroboration; strongly, urgently, emphatically, strictly, positively, peremptorily; as a reminder.
adj. Corroborative; urgent, pressing; emphatic, positive, peremptory; furnishing positive injunctions or directions (as a letter, &c.).
more: adj. 1. Emphatic; positive; absolute. 2. Urgent; pressing; imperative. tākīdī-chiṭṭī , n. f. A letter of injunction; a written mandate or direction. hukm-i-tākīdī , n. f. Peremptory order.

ety: takī = takya+nī = lī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ल+इका
def: s.f. A small pillow or cushion.
more: (from تکيه) s. f. A small pillow.

ety: inf. n. ii of عقر 'to wound'
def: s.f. Wounding much.
more: (inf. ii. of عقر) s. Wounding much.

def: s.m. = تکيه takya, q.v. s.m. A pillow, bolster, cushion; anything upon which one leans, prop, support; reliance, trust; the reserve of an army; a place of repose; the stand or abode of a faqīr
more: n. f. 1. See ṭikkī. 'Bīvī, bīvī, Īd āī.' 'Chal, harāmzādī! tujhe apnī dāl, ṭikyā se kām .' Wom. Prov. 'My lady, my lady, Īd festival's come!' 'What's that to you, wretch, mind your cooking, meddlesome!' 2. A small charcoal cake for the hukkā .
subs: (-ko), lit. 'To give (one) a pillow to recline against'; to receive with respect; — to lean or rest (upon or against)
(-par), To lean, rest, or recline (upon); to depend or rely (upon)
s.m. An expletive; a needless word or phrase repeatedly or habitually introduced into speech (such as the English 'do you see'; 'do you understand,' &c.)
(-par), To lean (upon or against), &c. = takya karnā (-par)

ety: prob. a dim. fr. t̤āq, q.v.
def: s.m. A fillet (esp. one worn under the head-dress); — a balcony; a cornice (see t̤āq).

ety: for A. تقية taqīyat, fem., inf. n. of تقي
def: s.m. Fearing God, fear (of God), piety; caution ( = taqwā); pious fraud or subterfuge (allowable in certain cases; e.g. in the case of a Shīa's visiting Mecca as a pilgrim, and calling himself a Sunnī).
s.m. A goat (particularly a he-goat that leads the flock)
more: n. m. A piece of flesh. Āëgā kuttā to pāëgā tikkā . Prov. When the dog comes he will get his share. (Who works gets). tikka boṭī k., yā uṛānā , v. n. To cut in pieces and distribute. tikke lagānā , Wom. v. n. To roast. Tere tikke lagāūṅ . Wom. I'll roast (and eat) you.
s. m. A goat. تکه ريش takka-rīsh, Beard on the chin only like a goat. تکه ريشي takka-rīsh-ī, s. f. The state of having beard on the chin only as the goat.
subs: s.m. Goat-beard, a beard on the chin only, like a goat's
s.f. The state of having beard on the chin only, as the goat.

def: s.m. The pot-herb Chenopodium album.

ety: S. स्तक्+ar = Prk. अडी = S. र+ई
def: s.f. Striking or knocking (against), running (against), collision, impact; pushing, push, jostling; butting, the knocking of head against head; shock, concussion, blow, knock; damage, loss; encounter, rencontre; competition, rivalry; equality, resemblance, comparison; rival, antagonist, match, equal
more: s. f. Shoving, pushing, push, shouldering, knocking against, collision, striking a blow, butt, butting, the knocking of head to head; equality. ٿکرپـﮩ‍اڙسي ليني ṭakkar pahāṛ se lenī, To enter the lists against an antagonist of greatly superiour force. ٿکر کهانا ṭakkar khānā, 1. To stumble. 2. To be dashed against any thing. 3. To meet with a loss or misfortune. ٿکر مارنا ṭakkar mārnā, 1. To knock against. 2. ( met. from the suppliantsʼ striking their heads on the ground) To pray. 3. To curse.
subs: v.n. To enter the lists against an adversary of greatly superior force (see ṭakkar lenā)
v.n. To suffer a blow, meet a shock, endure hardship, trials, &c., to bear up against loss, calamity, &c.; to stand aghast

(or ṭakkareṅ) khānā (-se), To knock, strike, run, or bump (against), to come into collision (with); to stumble; to be dashed (against); to fight or contend (with); to vie (with); to meet with a loss or misfortune
v.t. To knock or dash against, &c. = ṭakrānā, q.v.
(-se), To butt; to vie (with), &c. = ṭakkar khānā and ṭakkar mārnā

v.n. To knock (against), (met. from the suppliant's striking his head against the ground) to pray; to curse; to butt; to vie (with), to rival, emulate; to contend (against), to oppose; to strive, endeavour, use effort or exertion; to try in vain, exert one-self uselessly
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀝𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀭 (ṭakkara). Cognate with Punjabi ਟੱਕਰ (ṭakkar) / ٹَکَّر (ṭakkar), Gujarati ટક્કર (ṭakkar), Marathi टक्कर (ṭakkar), Bengali টক্কর (ṭokkor).

def: s.f. corr. of ṭakkar, q.v. s.f. Striking or knocking (against), running (against), collision, impact; pushing, push, jostling; butting, the knocking of head against head; shock, concussion, blow, knock; damage, loss; encounter, rencontre; competition, rivalry; equality, resemblance, comparison; rival, antagonist, match, equal
subs: v.n. To enter the lists against an adversary of greatly superior force (see ṭakkar lenā)
v.n. To suffer a blow, meet a shock, endure hardship, trials, &c., to bear up against loss, calamity, &c.; to stand aghast

(or ṭakkareṅ) khānā (-se), To knock, strike, run, or bump (against), to come into collision (with); to stumble; to be dashed (against); to fight or contend (with); to vie (with); to meet with a loss or misfortune
v.t. To knock or dash against, &c. = ṭakrānā, q.v.
(-se), To butt; to vie (with), &c. = ṭakkar khānā and ṭakkar mārnā

v.n. To knock (against), (met. from the suppliant's striking his head against the ground) to pray; to curse; to butt; to vie (with), to rival, emulate; to contend (against), to oppose; to strive, endeavour, use effort or exertion; to try in vain, exert one-self uselessly

ety: S. तर्कुट+कः
def: s.m. A spindle ( = takuʼā).
more: Tir. n. m. S. तर्कु A spindle. takle ke se bal nikālnā , v. a. To take one's nonsense out of him; to settle or put one to rights; bring one to his senses. Bal tere takle kī mānind nikāle koī! Kyā burī sūt kī anṭī ye aṭerī bāṅdī! Wom. Raṅgīṅ. I would your airs, like spindle-crook, somebody would take out! So badly you have rolled this skein, slave girl, it's all about! sūkh-kar taklā h . v. n. To be worn to a spindle; to be as thin as a lath, reduced to mere skin and bone.
s. m. A spindle. See تکوا and ٿکوا.

ety: S. तर्कुट+इका
def: s.f. A weaver's reed (cf. taklā).
more: n. f. 1. A water. 2. A spangle, or ornament worn on the forehead. 1. Ṭikulī na pahiroṅ bālam! morā jiā ke janjāl . Mag. song. No spangle, husband, will I wear, the men do plague me so wherever I appear. (The spangles provoke so much admiration of her!) 2. ' Rājā jī ke bāg meṅ ṭikulyā re bikāë,' 'Le de, le de, re devarvā! morā jiā lalchāë .' Mag. song. Such pretty spangles in the Rajā's garden I've just seen, 'Buy, buy me, deora mine, to have them I've such longing keen.' 3. Buṛhvā bhatār par tīn ṭikulī! Bhoj. Three spangles for an old husband! 4. Māth mise chalalo re bābā kā sagarvāṅ herailī majedār, re birogio, Goṛ torāṅ lāgī le, kheṅvaṅṭa re malhavā, ehī re sagarvāṅ ḍālū mahā jāl, re birogī! Bhoj. song. My head earth-rubbed, here in my father's tank, ah wretched me! My luscious spangle dropped, thy large net, steersman, spread, prythee! 3. A round piece of metal to close a hole.
(see تکلا taklā ) s. f. A weaverʼs reel.

ety: inf. n. ii of قلب
def: s.f. Turning (a thing) upside down, or inside out; inverting; changing; inversion; conversion, change; transposition; transmutation.
more: (inf. ii. of قلب) s. m. Conversion, inversion, change, transposition.

ety: inf. n. ii of قلد 'to fold' (one thing over another)
def: s.f. Investing (with authority), investiture; imitation, representation; mimicing, mimicry; a counterfeiting; forgery
more: n. f. قلد Putting a rope round the neck. 1. Imitation; copy; representation. 2. Counterfeiting.
(inf. ii. of قلد) s. f. Imitation, counterfeiting, forgery. 2. Investiture. تقليدکرنا taḳlīd karnā, To imitate, counterfeit, mimick, ape.
subs: (-kī), To imitate, copy, mimic, ape; to represent; to feign, to counterfeit.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تقلید (taqlīd), from Arabic تَقْلِيد (taqlīd).

def: adj. Imitated; forged; counterfeit; false (as stone, &c.).
more: adj. 1. Imitated; copied. 2. Forged; counterfeit; false. mohar-i-taqlīdī ; G. G. A counterfeit seal.
adj. Imitated, forged, false (as, stone, &c.), counterfeit.

ety: inf. n. ii of کلف 'to attend carefully (to)'
def: s.f. Ceremony; the imposition of a burthen (upon); burden, difficulty, trouble, distress, inconvenience; molestation, injury, annoyance, hardship, grievance; suffering, ailment, affliction
more: n. f. 1. Annoyance; trouble; difficulty; infliction; nuisance. 2. Grievance; hardship; suffering; injury. 3. Burden; load; plague. taklīf uṭhānā, yā pānā , v. n. To suffer. taklīf denā, yā pahoṅchānā , v. a. To inflict pain; cause trouble, etc. taklīf k . v. n. To take trouble. Āp taklīf na kīje . Dont trouble yourself. taklīf meṅ , adv. In straitened circumstances. amr bāis-i-taklīf-i-ām . G. G. A public nuisance.

(inf. ii. of کلف) s. f. 1. Ceremony. 2. Imposition of a burthen. 3. Trouble, difficulty, molestation, distress, inconvenience, ailment, affliction, annoyance. (Plur. تکليفات taklīfāt ). تکليف دينا taklīf denā, To annoy, molest, incommode, trouble. تکليف کرنا taklīf karnā, To take the trouble.
subs: v.n. To experience trouble, &c.; to suffer
(-ko), and taklīf denā (-ko), To inflict pain (on), to trouble, incommode; to annoy, molest, &c.
v.n. To take the trouble, to trouble oneself
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْلِیف (taklīf), borrowed from Arabic تَكْلِيف (taklīf), from كَلَّفَ (kallafa).

ety: inf. n. ii of قلّ 'to be little or few'
def: s.f. Diminishing, diminution, reduction; causing to look less; paucity.
more: (inf. ii. of قلّت) s. f. Diminishing, reduction, causing to look less.

ety: inf. n. ii of کمل 'to be perfect'
def: s.f. Perfecting; finishing, completing; perfection, excellence; completion; accomplishment; — authentication, authority, validity
more: n. f. کمل completed. 1. See پوراپن 1, 2, 4, 5. 2. Authentication; validity; authority. takmīl pānā , v. n. To be completed; finished. takmīl-i-tamassuk , Execution of bond. takmīl-i-rahn , Foreclosure of mortgage. takmīl k., takmīl ko pauṅhchānā , v. a. 1. To execute; complete. 2. To authenticate; to give authority to. adam-takmīl , G. G. Non-execution.
(inf. ii. of کمل) s. f. Completing, finishing, perfection, excellence.
subs: v.n. To be finished or completed
takmīl-ko pahuṅćānā, v.t. To finish, complete, bring to a conclusion or termination, to execute; to give authority (to), to authenticate.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَكْمِيل (takmīl).

ety: for A. تکملة takmilat, fem., inf. n. ii of کمل; see kamāl
def: s.m. Perfection; completion (cf. takmīl).
more: (inf. ii. of کمل) s. m. Perfection, completion.

ety: tāk˚ = Prk. तक्क(इ), or तक्के(इ) = S. तर्कय(ति), rt. तर्क्
def: v.t. To look at, view, gaze on, behold; to stare at; to watch for; — v.n. To peep, spy, watch; to aim (at, -par).
more: v. a. 1. To look at; stare at; be on the watch; wait for. 2. To lie in ambush. Billī ko dekho, kyā tāk lagāë baiṭhī hai!
v. a. To stare at, to aim, peep, see, spy, watch, view, behold, look, look for, attend.

def: v.t. = taknā or tāknā, q.v. v.t. To look at, view, gaze on, behold; to stare at; to watch for; — v.n. To peep, spy, watch; to aim (at, -par).

ety: S. टङ्क+र or ल+कः
def: s.m. The ankle ( = ṭaḵẖnā).

ety: see tāknā, and tāk
def: v.t. To look at, peep at, peer into; to look steadfastly at, to fix the gaze upon, to gaze or stare at; to watch or wait for, to look out for, to expect; to look wistfully or longingly at or to; — v.n. To look, peep, spy, watch; to aim (at), take aim; to point (as a dog); to be looked at; to be stared at
more: v. n. 1. To stare; gaze; strain one's eyes. 1. Mūṅh taknā . 2. Nadīdoṅ kī tarah tak rahā hai . He stares as though he never saw the like before. 3. Thakā ūṅṭ sarāë take . Prov. The tired camel looks for the sarāë (inn). 4. Ap to saiyāṅ pār utar gaë; Maiṅ taktī kī taktī rahī . Wom. My love is borne across the main; I only gaze and stare in vain. 2. To cast wanton looks at one; to ogle. Kisī kī bahū beṭī ko taknā achchhā nahīṅ . It is not proper to stare at others' wives and daughters. 3. To aim; watch; take an aim. 4. To lie in wait for; to point. Tūtī pāleṅ chūtyā, aur āshaq pāleṅ lāl, Kabūtar pāleṅ choṭṭe jo takeṅ parāyā māl . Prov. Who rosefinds keep are fools, the amadabat's the lover's choice, The pigeon-fanciers are all thieves, in captures they rejoice. 5. To wait for; expect; look wishfully. 6. To look to; depend or lean on. Jo parāyā hāth takegā, woh kyā kamāëgā? 7. To have recou…

v. n. 1. To stop; stay; halt; tarry; lodge; abide. Āo musāfar, mere ṭikyo, khūb safā makkān, Mere bagaṛ meṅ nīm kī chhāyāṅ, koṭhe aur dālān. Bhaṭyārī inn-keepers' cry. Come, traveller, in my inn abide, my room is clean, you 'll find, Verandah, inner-room, and nīm tree's grateful shade combined. 2. To stick (in the mud, sand bank). 3. To continue; last. Paisā kabhī nahīṅ ṭiktā . Prov. saying. Fortune never stays. 4. To live; survive; to be living. Ṭuk ṭikve ke kārne, ketak ṭhak ṭhak hoë, Ketak ṭhak ṭhak kar gaë, ṭuk ṭik rahā na koë . Dohā. A little while to stay what labors men essay, What labors men essay, yet scarcely do they stay.
v. n. 1. To be sewn. 2. To be studded, embellished.
1. v. a. To look at, to look, to observe, to aim at, to watch. 2. v. n. To be looked at, to be stared at, to look.
subs: v.t. (intens. of taknā), To keep the eye fixed on, to look steadfastly at, &c.

ety: i.q. ṭaṅknā, q.v.
def: v.n. To be stitched; to be studded with gems or embroidery, to be embellished or adorned.
more: 1. v. n. To be stitched. 2. v. a. (dakh.) See تاکنا tāknā.

def: s.f. = tag-o-dau, q.v.s.v. tag.
more: n. f. H. dauṛ dhūp . 1. Close pursuit or search. See پيروي (1, 3, 4.) 2. Anxiety ( udheṛ-bun ); care; concern.

ety: S. स्तन्+क+उल+कः
def: A fillip, tap; the sound of a drum; the treble end of the Moorish kettle-drum (cf. ṭaṅkār, ṭankor).

n. f. 1. The sound of a drum. Ṭakoroṅ meṅ naubat kī shahnā kī dhun, Sughaṛ sunne-vāloṅ ko kartī thī sun . Mīr. Hasan. 2. The treble end of a Moorish kettledrum. 3. Fomentation. Poṭliyoṅ kī ṭakoreṅ do .
s.m. A hot cloth applied to an afflicted part; a cataplasm, poultice.
more: s. m. A cataplasm, a poultice.
s. f. 1. The sound of a drum. 2. A fillip, tap.

ety: S. स्तन्+क+उल+कः
def: A fillip, tap; the sound of a drum; the treble end of the Moorish kettle-drum (cf. ṭaṅkār, ṭankor).
s. (dakh.) Small kettledrums: see زيربم zer-bam (Qanoon-e-islam).
v. a. To foment.

ety: see ṭakor
def: v.t. To foment.
adj. = tagṛā, q.v. adj. Stout, strong, robust.
adj. Stout and strong; robust.
more: n. m. H. ṭuk a little. 1. Portion; part; fraction; division. 2. Piece; fragment; plot. 3. Cut; slice; bit; scrap; shred. Nānā ke ṭukṛe khāve, dādā kā potā kahlāve . Prov. He eats his maternal grandfather's bread, and he is called a grandson by the father's side. (One does the work, another gets the name.) 4. Chapter; section; paragraph; clause. 5. Morsel ( kaur ); bread; the common necessaries of life; livelihood ( rozī ); living. Miyāṅ, kyoṅ lage lagāë ṭukṛe ko khote ho? ṭukṛe ṭukṛe k . v. a. 1. To break in pieces; to analyze; take, pull, or pick to pieces. 2. To cut up; tear to tatters; rip up; chaw up; chop or make mince-meat of ( qīmā k .). ṭukṛe k . v. n. To break; parcel out; divide in shares; to divide; partition; portion; apportion. ṭukṛā denā , v. a. 1. To give a scrap (of bread, etc). 2. To allot in small shares; dole out. chāṅd, parī, yā shafaq kā ṭukṛā , adj. Beautiful; hands…

def: s.m. Buttermilk with a fourth part water
more: n. f. 1. Knocking or running against; collision; push; jostling; knock; bang; impact. Shākir ko shakkar, mūzī ko ṭakkar . Prov. For the grateful, rewards, for the oppressor blows. 2. Box; blow; shock; concussion; percussion; butt. Jab tak ṭakkar nahīṅ lagtī ādmī nahīṅ bantā . Until the lash on him is laid a man he never will be made. 3. Encounter; rencounter; conflict; contest. 4. Damage; loss. Das rupae kī aur ṭakkar lagī . 5. Competition; rivalry; race; match. 6. A rival; antagonist; an equal. ṭakkar jhelnā, yā lagnā , v. n. 1. To stand aghast; to meet a shock, blow, etc.; to bear up against loss, calamity, etc.; to stem the torrent. 2. To endure hardship, pain, etc. ṭakkar khānā , v. n. 1. ( ṭakrānā ) To knock, strike, or bump against; to collide; come into collision; clash; butt. 2. To fight or contend with; try conclusions with; measure swords. 3. To suffer any loss or damage. ṭakka…
n. f. A thick hard cake. Apnā ṭikkaṛ meṅ se tanī hamro dīhā ho, nanbāī bhaiyā . Mag. Give me a small piece of your bread, brother baker!

def: v. n. See ṭakkar khānā . 1. Rel se rel ṭakrā gaī . To grope in a dark place or passage. Ṭakrāte huë phirte haiṅ ham kūche meṅ uske, Kyā kījye, darvāza idhar band, udhar band! Inshā.

ety: fr. ṭakkar
def: v.t. To knock (a body) against (another), to bring into collision; to knock together the heads of two people; to butt; to dash together; to dash against (syn. ṭakkar khilānā); — v.n. To knock (against), &c. ( = ṭakkar khānā, q.v.); to go along stumbling, to grope in a dark place or passage
more: (from ٿکر) 1. v. a. To knock together the heads of two people, to dash the head on something, to dash together, to butt. 2. v. n. To grope in a dark place or passage.

def: s.f. Repeating often; repetition; tautology; the chorus or burthen of a song; question, dispute; objection, controversy, contention, altercation, wrangling, wrangle, cavil
more: n. f. کر repeating. See an ban , 1 to 3. Repetition; iteration; the chorus or burden of a song. Zarā takrār se kaho . Just repeat the line of the song. 2. Question; dispute; point at issue. 3. Objection; controversy ( bahs , 1, 2.). takrār k . v. a. 1. To quarrel; wrangle; squabble. 2. To object; remonstrate; dispute. 3. To differ from; join issue; contend for; plead; argue; debate; cavil; litigate. khās takrār , n. f. The chief objection; the real question; the main issue; the material point.
(inf. ii. of کرّ) s. f. Repetition, tautology, altercation, contention, dispute, wrangle, wrangling, cavil. تکرارکرنا takrār karnā, To dispute, debate, argue, wrangle, cavil, repeat.
subs: (-kī and -par), To repeat; to argue or debate (upon or about, to object (to), remonstrate; to cavil (at); to dispute, contend, to carry on an altercation, to wrangle, quarrel.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْرَار (takrār), from Arabic تَكْرار (takrār).

def: adj. Contentious, disputatious, fond of argument, importuning, troublesome; wrangling, quarrelsome; litigious; — s.m. Caviller, carper; wrangler, &c.
more: adj. 1. Contentious; disputatious; fond of argument; litigious. 2. Wrangling; quarrelsome.
adj. Importuning, seizing, troublesome, fond of argument, wrangler, wrangling, caviller, carper.

ety: inf. n. ii of قرب 'to draw near'
def: s.f. Giving access (to), causing to approach, bringing near; approaching; approximation, proximity; approach, access; commending, recommending, mentioning (anyone) to another before meeting; recommendation, mention; occasion, conjuncture; festive occasion, festival; ceremony, rite; cause, means; appearance, probability; pretence, motive
more: n. f. قرب came near 1. Proximity; approximation. 2. Occasion; urgency. Kis taqrib se āë ho? What brings you here? 3. Ceremony; rite. 4. Appliances; means.

(inf. ii. of قرب) s. f. 1. Bringing near, approximation, approaching, proximity, access. 2. Recommendation, mentioning any one to another before meeting. 3. Occasion, conjunction, cause, pretence, motive, probability. تقريب کرنا taḳrīb karnā, To commend, to recommend, to prepare a person for the reception of another.
subs: v.t. To bring (one) near (another); to commend, recommend; to prepare (one) for the reception (of another).

ety: acc. of taqrīb
def: adv. Near to, nearly, approximately, about.
ex: میری جیب میں تَقْرِیباً ۵۰۰ رُوپَے ہَیں
merī jeb mẽ taqrīban 500 rūpai ha͠i
I have around 500 rupees in my pocket

Those guys have been working there for around 6 years
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْرِیباً (taqrīban), borrowed from Arabic تَقْرِيبًا (taqrīban).

def: s.f. pl. (of taqrīb), Occasions; festive occasions, festivals, ceremonies, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of کرم 'to be noble or honourable,' &c.
def: s.f. Honouring, revering; treating with honour or respect; honour, respect, reverence
more: (inf. ii. of کرم) s. f. 1. Honour, reverence, respect. 2. Treating with respect, honouring.
subs: (-kī), To treat (one) with honour, or respect; to honour, respect, revere.

ety: inf. n. ii of قرّ 'to pour' (words into another's ear); cf. taqarrur
def: s.f. Speaking, discoursing; relating; explaining; confirming; speech, discourse, statement, declaration, assertion, relation, recital, narrative, account, detail; exposition; avowal, confession; ascertainment; confirmation
more: n. f. قر pouring words into another's ear. See بيان 1. Discourse; speech; lecture; oration. 2. Argument; reasoning.
(inf. ii. of قرّ or قرار) s. f. Confession, avowal, exposition, declaration, assertion, ascertainment, relation, recital, detail, narration, confirmation. تقريرکرنا taḳrīr karnā, To relate.
subs: (-kī), To speak, discourse (of); to relate, recite, declare, assert, &c.

ety: acc. of taqrīr
def: adv. By word of mouth, orally.
adv. By word of mouth, orally.
more: adj. Disputatious; argumentative. taqrīrī , G. G. adv. By word of mouth. taqrīryā , adj. Talkative; loquacious. khush-taqrīr , adj. Eloquent.

def: adj. Addicted to talking much, talkative, loquacious; fond of arguing, disputatious.
adj. Addicted to talking much, talkative, loquacious; fond of arguing, disputatious.

ety: inf. n. ii of قرس 'to be very cold'
def: s.f. Chilling, cooling; congealing.
more: (inf. ii. of قرس) s. f. Cooling, congealing.

ety: inf. n. ii of قرظ 'to praise' (the living)
def: s.f. lit. 'Praising the living' (justly or unjustly); reviewing, review.

ety: S. टङ्क+शाला
def: s.f. A mint, assay office
more: s. f. A mint. ٿکسال چڙهنا ṭaksāl chaṛhnā, To be educated. ٿکسال باهر ṭaksāl-bāhar, adj. Uneducated, unpolished, rude, unclassical. ٿکسال کا کهوٿا ṭaksāl kā khoṭā, adj. Spoilt in education.
subs: adj. & s.m. Not bearing the mint stamp (coin); unidiomatic, unchaste, obsolete, barbarous (words, &c.); uneducated, unpolished, rude; — coin not stamped at a mint; &c.
(-par), To bear the mint stamp; to be educated or polished; to be void of shame, to be brazen-faced

ṭaksāl-kā khoṭā, adj. lit. 'Broken or spoiled in the mint'; spoiled or mutilated in the birth; spoiled or corrupted in the parents' home, or in early training, &c.; base-born; ill-bred, badly trained or educated.

def: n. f. S. टङ्कक शाला. Assay office; mint. ṭaksāl-bāhar , adj. Unidiomatic, obsolete. Ṭaksāl bāhar muhāvre na bolo . Dont use base-coined words which have not the true ring. ṭaksāl chaṛhnā , v. n. To be brazen-faced; to lose all sense of shame. Jān Sāhab. ṭaksāl kā khoṭā , adj. Base-born; badly taught, ill-bred. ṭaksālī , n. m. An officer of the mint; mint-master. ṭaksā'lī , adj. 1. Real; true; right. 2. Sound ( aslī , 2). 3. See asl , 5. ṭaksālī zabān . Pure language; good idiom; true coinage of the mint.

ety: ṭaksāl+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to, or coined in, a mint; of true ring or value; true, genuine, real; sound; pure, chaste, current (speech); — an officer of the mint, mint-master; coiner in a mint.
more: s. m. Officers of the mint.

def: s.m. = ṭaksālī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Of or pertaining to, or coined in, a mint; of true ring or value; true, genuine, real; sound; pure, chaste, current (speech); — an officer of the mint, mint-master; coiner in a mint.
more: s. m. Officers of the mint.

def: s.f. corr. of next, q.v.
s.m. Cutter, wood-cutter; carpenter ( = baṛhaʼī); one of the principal nāgas or serpents of Pātāla; a snake of a middle size and of a red colour.
s.m. Cutting, paring, peeling, planing.
s.m. A wood-cutter, carpenter
subs: s.f. Name of a city of the Gandhāras in the Panjāb, the Taxila of Ptolemy.

def: s.f. A carpenter's adze, an axe, &c.

ety: inf. n. ii of کشف 'to be dense,' &c.
def: s.f. Rendering thick or dense, condensing.
(inf. ii. of کثف) s. f. Rendering thick or dense, condensation.

ety: inf. n. ii of قسم 'to divide into parts'
def: s.f. Dividing, distributing, sharing; division, distribution, partition, allotment; division (in Arith.)
more: n. f. from قسم dividing. 1. Division; partition; allotment (بانتَ 1). 2. ( Arith. ) Division (H. bhāg ). taqsīm-i-tarka , G. G. Distribution of property. taqsīm-i-jadīd . Repartition; new division. taqsīm-i-khāngī . A private partition. taqsīm-i-dāvā . Splitting of claims. taqsīm sarmāya , G. G. A distribution of stock or capital. taqsīm-i-farebī ; H. chhal baṭvārah . A fraudulent partition. taqsīm kār-pardāzān-i-pulīs , G. G. Distribution of the police force. taqsīm k . v. a. See bāṅ/?/nā. taqsīm-i-māl . Division or distribution of property. taqsīm-i-mukhtasar ; S. संक्षेप भाग n. f. Short division. taqsīm-i-murakkab ; S. मिश्र भाग n. f. Compound division. taqsīm-nāmah , A record of division of property ( baṭvārā ). taqsīm-i-virsā . A partition of inherited property.

(inf. ii. of قسم) s. f. 1. Dividing, division, distributing, distribution, sharing, partition. 2. Division (in arithmetick). تقسيم کرنا taḳsīm karnā, To divide, share, distribute, part, portion, apportion, assort.
subs: s.f. Division of land between co-sharers who are jointly bound for the payment of the revenue
s.f. A fresh division or partition, redistribution
(with acc. or gen. of first, and loc. of second object), To divide, distribute (among or over, -meṅ, or -par), to share, part, portion, apportion, assort
s.m. A deed or record of division of property ( = bāṅṭ patra)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْسِیم (taqsīm), borrowed from Arabic تَقْسِيم (taqsīm), from قَسَّمَ (qassama).

def: adj. Divisible, liable to partition; according to allotment.
more: adj. Divisible; liable to partition; according to allotment.

ety: inf. n. ii of قصر 'to shorten'
def: s.f. Defect, failure, omission, shortcoming; mistake, error, fault, offence, crime misdemeanour; guilt, blame
more: n. f. قصر diminishing. 1. Omission; failure; neglect. 2. A mistake; error; fault. 3. Offence; misdemeanor; crime; guilt. taqsīr-vār, taqsīr-mand , adj. 1. Blameable; faulty. 2. Guilty; culpable; criminal. be-taqsīr ; adj. Blameless; innocent.
(inf. ii. of قصر or قصور) s. f. Fault, errour, crime, offence. (Plur. تقصيرات taḳṣīrāt ). تقصيروار taḳṣīr-wār, Blamable, faulty, criminal, guilty, convict, culpable.
subs: taqṣīr-wār, adj. Blamable, culpable, faulty, criminal, guilty.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic تَقْصِير (taqṣīr).

ety: inf. n. ii of کشر 'to be much, or abundant,' &c.
def: s.f. Multiplying, increasing, augmenting, enlarging.

ety: inf. n. v of کسر 'to break'
def: s.f. Breaking to pieces, shattering; carrying (figures in arithmetic); dividing so as to produce a fraction.
more: (inf. ii. of کسر) s. f. 1. Breaking to pieces. 2. Carrying (figures, in arithmetick). 3. Dividing so as to produce a fraction.

def: (inf. ii. of کثر) s. f. Augmenting, enlarging, increasing.
s.f. pl. (of taqṣīr), Shortcomings, omissions, errors, failings; offences, crimes.

ety: onomat.
def: s.m. The sound of feet, &c. ( = ṭak-ṭak).
more: n. f. S. तिक to go. lit . Go! go! A call to bullocks to go on. 1. Tik tik karte thak gaë, par ṭaṭṭū na chalā . 2. Āā jāne, tik tik jāne, kahe sune se rahe khaṛā, Kaheṅ Kabīr, suno bhaī sādhoṅ, yā mānas se bail bhalā . Kabīr.

def: s.f. Rapping, rap, &c. = ṭhak-ṭhak, q.v. (cf. tak-tak). adj. See s.v. thak. s.m. A boundary pillar built of masonry (in opp. to ḍhūʼā, 'a mud pillar').
more: s. f. See تک تک tak-tak.

def: s.m. = ṭak-ṭakī, q.v. s.f. A carpenter's level (com. ṭakṭakī).
v.t. = ṭhakṭhakānā, q.v. v.t. To knock, rap, tap, pat, sound (cf. khaṭkhaṭānā).

ety: tak, redupl.+Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A carpenter's level (com. ṭakṭakī).
more: E. n. f. 1. A steadfast look; stare; gaze. 1. Man. ' Nain ṭakāṭak lag rahe, chhuṭī na dil kī bhūk, Bin chābhai ras nā mile, kyā nārī kyā ūkh .' Woman. ' Nainā ṭakāṭak dūr hiṅ bhalā, yā jag māhīṅ ke rīt, Ghan umṛat sobhat bhalo, barsat kādū kīṅch . Tir. 'I gaze and gaze with fixed look, my heart still longs in vain, Unsqueezed no juice you get, be 't woman or a sugarcane.' 'To gaze from far is much the best, the world hath so decreed, The hanging clouds are fair to see, the rains but puddle breed.' 2. Gaë din ṭikṭikī ke bāṅdhne ke, Ab āṅkheṅ rahtī haiṅ do do pahar band . The days of ogling are gone by, For hours these eyes now closed up lie. 2. The triangle; a tripod. Ṭikṭikī se bāṅdh dīyā. ṭukur ṭukur dekhnā , v. a. To stare; gaze; look steadfastly.

ety: inf. n. ii of قطع 'to cut off'
def: s.f. Cutting into parts, dissection; scanning (of verse); the cæsura or pause (in reading poetry)
more: (inf. ii. of قطع) s. f. 1. The cæsura or pause in reading poetry. 2. Scanning of verse. 3. Cutting into parts, dissection.
wety: From Arabic تَقْطِيع (taqṭīʕ), verbal noun of قَطَعَ (qaṭaʕa).

ety: inf. n. ii of قطر 'to drop'
def: s.f. Causing to come away drop by drop; distilling; distillation; passing (urine, &c.) in drops; strangury; diabetes; gonorrhœa.
more: (inf. ii. of قطر) s. f. Distilling, dropping (particles of urine), strangury.

def: n. f. Scanning. taqtī k .; H. chhand-mātrā-ginnā , v. a. To scan.
s.m. = takuʼā, q.v. s.m. A spindle ( = taklā).
more: s. m. A spindle. See تکوا.

ety: S. तर्कु+कः
def: s.m. A spindle ( = taklā).
more: s. m. A spindle. See تکلا taklā.

def: s.m. Fear of God, piety; preserving or guarding oneself from sin; abstinence; continence
more: n. m. قوي Abstinence; chastity; sobriety; piety.
s. m. Piety, abstinence, the fear of God. اهل تقويٰٰ ahl-i-taḳwạ̄, Pious.
(inf. v. of قوي) s. f. Assisting, (particularly, tenants by advances, remitting rents, &c.).
subs: adj. & s.m. Pious, devout; — the pious; a pious man, one who fears God.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْویٰ (taqwā), borrowed from Arabic تَقْوَى (taqwā).

ety: tak˚ = tāk˚ = Prk. तक्क˚ = S. तर्क+wāhī = wāʼī = Prk. आव+अइआ = S. आपि+ता+इका
def: s.f. Looking sharp after, watching, overseeing, superintending.
more: s. f. Watching, overseeing, superintending, looking sharp after (as after a silversmith).

ety: inf. n. ii of قوم 'to be standing or erect'
def: s.f. Making straight or erect; setting in order, ordering, regulating, adjusting, directing aright; fixing, determining, settling; estimating, rating; adjustment, proportion, symmetry; — an almanac, a calendar
more: n. f. قوم making straight. See پترا taqvīm-i-pārīnah , n. m. An old or useless thing.
(inf. ii. of قوم or قيام) s. f. An almanack, ephemeris, calendar.
s. f. Causing to be straight or erect.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَقْوِیم (taqwīm), from Arabic تَقْوِيم (taqwīm).

ety: inf. n. ii of کون 'to be'
def: s.f. Causing to be, giving a being or existence (to); originating; creating.
more: (inf. ii. of کون) s. f. Causing to be, making to exist.

ety: for A. تقوية, inf. n. ii of قوي
def: s.f. Strengthening, confirming, establishing; strength; corroboration; invigoration; support, aid; assurance, confidence; comfort; trust, reliance
more: n. f. قوي strengthening. Corroboration; support.
(inf. ii. of قوي) s. f. Corroboration, strengthening, establishing, confirming, confidence, strength, assurance, invigoration, support, comfort, reliance, trust, aid.
subs: (-kī), To strengthen, confirm; to sustain, support, back; to inspirit, encourage, &c.

def: s.m. A pillow, bolster, cushion; anything upon which one leans, prop, support; reliance, trust; the reserve of an army; a place of repose; the stand or abode of a faqīr
more: n. m. 1. A pillow; bolster ( gāo-takyā ). Takye ke nīche rakh dīyā . He has put it under his pillow (and forgotten it). 2. Prop; back; support. 3. The abode of a faqīr . 4. A reserve of an army. 5. Reliance; trust. Takyā kīye baiṭhā hūṅ. takye-dār , n. m. A Mah. religious mendicant; a faqīr. Bābā, takye-dār ko bhī koī paisā! Bestow a pice, Sir, on the devotee! takyā denā , E. v. n. lit . To give one his pillow. To receive one with respect. takyā k. yā lagānā , v. n. To lean, rest or rely upon. takyā-kalām, sukhan-takyā , n. m. An expletive; a needless repetition, a habitual or favorite phrase, as 'do you see,' 'do you understand.' gal-takyā , n. m. A pillow for the cheek.

s. m. 1. A pillow, bolster. 2. A faḳīrʼs stand. 3. The reserve of an army. 4. Reliance. تکيه دار takiya-dār, A faḳīr, a dervis. تکيه دينا takiya denā, To receive respectfully. تکيه کرنا takiya karnā, To support, to encourage. تکيه کسي پرکرنا takiya kisī par karnā, To depend upon. تکيه کلام takiya-kalām, or سخن تکيه suḵẖan-takiya, s. m. A cant word introduced into oneʼs conversation without any meaning, an expletive. تکيه لگانا takiya lagānā, To lean, to bolster. تکيه نشين takiya-nishīn, A faḳīr, a dervis.
subs: (-ko), lit. 'To give (one) a pillow to recline against'; to receive with respect; — to lean or rest (upon or against)
(-par), To lean, rest, or recline (upon); to depend or rely (upon)
s.m. An expletive; a needless word or phrase repeatedly or habitually introduced into speech (such as the English 'do you see'; 'do you understand,' &c.)
(-par), To lean (upon or against), &c. = takya karnā (-par)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَکْیَه (takya).

ety: inf. n. ii of کذب 'to lie'
def: s.f. Accusing of falsehood, giving (one) the lie
more: (inf. ii. of کذب) s. f. Accusing of falsehood, giving the lie.

ety: S. तल्लः
def: s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).
more: n. m. S. ताल 1. Beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime. Tīn tāl par ḍholak bāje, ek tāl par ḍanḍā, Jaun tāl par jogin nāche, wohī tāl par lauṅḍā. Jogīrā . 2. A pair of cymbals. 3. Clapping the hands together. 4. The glass or pebble of an eye glass. tāl ṭhoknā , v. n. See kham ṭhoknā . tāl se be-tāl honā , v. n. To be out of tune. tāl mel , n. m. See اتفاق (1 to 5, 7). tāl mel khānā , v. n. To agree. See ittefāq honā . terah-tālī , n. f. 1. A perfect dancer. 2. An accomplished coquette. 3. A very artful woman.

n. f. 1. A stack (اتَالا 2.). 2. Evasion ( ālā bālā 1.) 3. A bell attached to the necks of cattle. ṭāl mārnā , v. a. To give a twist to the beam in weighing. ṭāl k . v. n. 1. To set up a ṭāl or heap of wood, straw, etc. for sale. 2. To postpone; put off; delay.

s. m. 1. ( ताल ) Beating time (in musick), musical time or measure, chime. 2. Slapping or clapping the hands together or against the arms, &c. 3. A musical instrument of bell-metal or brass (a sort of cymbal) played with a stick. 4. ( ताड ) The palm tree. (Borassus flabelliformis). 5. ( तडाग ) A pond. تال دينا tāl denā, To chime: to slap the hands on the arms before fighting. تال مارنا ياٿهوکنا tāl-mārnā or -ṭhoknā, To strike the hand against the arms preparatory to wrestling.
s. m. Cymbals used by devotees, and frequently an accompaniment to the t̤āʼịfas (Qanoon-e-islam).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताल (tāla).

ety: S. अअट्टालरोलर?
def: s.f. A station or place for storing wood, grain, grass, and the like, and where it is sold; a store of wood, grain, chaff, &c.; a stack (of wood), heap (of grain, &c.), a rick
more: 1. verbal n. f. (from ٿالنا) Evasion, putting aside, putting off or out of the way, rejecting a request. 2. s. f. A heap (of grain, &c.), a stack, a rick. 3. Baldness from age. 4. A turn or trick in wrestling. ٿال کرنا ṭāl karna, 1. To put off. 2. To heap. ٿال مارنا ṭāl mārnā, 1. To heap. 2. To turn the scale fraudulently in weighing. ٿال مٿال ṭāl maṭāl, Fraud, deceit: see ٿال مٿول.
subs: (-kī), To set up a ṭāl or store of wood, &c.; to make a stack, heap, or rick (of)
(-kī), To heap up, to heap; — to turn the scale fraudulently (in weighing), to give the beam a twist (and thus diminish the true weight).

ety: S. ताली
def: s.f. A small bell (attached to the neck of an ox, or a cow, or an elephant).
v. n. To get out of the way, to disappear, to vanish, to pass away.

ety: v.n. of ṭālnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Passing over, going beyond (a fixed time); putting off, deferring; evasion, subterfuge, shuffling, prevarication, chicane; putting or turning aside, putting out of the way, deterring; rejecting (a request); — a turn or trick in wrestling; — baldness from age)
subs: ṭāl-maṭāl, ṭālam-ṭol, ṭalmaṭol, s.m. Postponement; disappointment, evasion, &c. = ṭāl
v.n. To practise delay, to put off; to practise evasion or subterfuge; to temporize; to shuffle, prevaricate, &c. (see ṭālnā).

def: adj. 1. Bitter (H. kaṛvā ). 2. Acrid; acrimonious. talkh k . v. a. 1. To embitter; to make bitter. 2. To throw cold water on; to damp. talkh yā kaṛvā mizāj , adj. Ill-tempered.
more: adj. 1. Bitter, acrimonious, acrid, rancid. 2. Malicious, sorrowful. تلخ رو talḵẖ-rū, Morose, surly, stern. تلخ دانه talḵẖ-dāna, Darnel, tares. تلخ کام talḵẖ-kām, adj. Bitter in palate. تلخ کامي talḵẖ-kām-ī, s. f. Bitterness to the palate. تلخ کرنا talḵẖ karnā, To imbitter or make bitter. تلخ گو talḵẖ-go, adj. Plain spoken, harsh. تلخ مزاج talḵẖ-mizāj, adj. Ill-tempered.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلخ (talx).

ety: Prk. तालओ; S. तालकः
def: s.m. Look, padlock
more: n. m. S. तालक A lock. tālā toṛnā . To break open or force a lock; break open; break into a house. tālā bhiṛnā yā lagnā , v. n. To be locked up (a house), ruined. Bhiṛ jāve Rām tero tālā! Mo pe pīsan kā dukhṛā ḍālā! Wom. Rus. song. So be't your house destroyed—a lock put on outside! To the task of grinding corn each day, O Ram, you have me tied. tālā kunjī , n. m. A boys' game.

n. m. 1. Evasion; putting off; shuffling; equivocation; fencing. 2. Prevarication; tergiversation; chicanery. ṭālam-ṭol k . v. a. 1. To put off ( ālā bālā k . 1.). 2. To dodge; parry; temporize; fence; sham. 3. To evade; elude by stratagem; prevaricate; equivocate.
subs: v.n. To be locked up, to be closed (a house or shop)
(-kā), To break open or to force a lock; to break into (a house)
(-meṅ), To put a padlock (on), to fasten with a padlock ( = qufl lagānā)
wety: From Sanskrit तालक (tālaka).

def: s.f. The leathern fence worn by archers on the left arm.
more: n. m. Cor. of A. tilā gold. The gold lace border of a turban.

n. m. S. तल 1. The bottom; lowest part; keel. 2. The sole of a boot or shoe. 1. Merī jūtī ke tale se! Wom. 2. Baṛe-vāle * se mochan kyoṅ laṛe hai, Tale † se kām donoṅ kā chale hai . Jest. Mazāq . 3. A layer; coating. Talā de-kar hāṅḍī chaṛhā do . Wom.

n. m. (Slang). Impetus sive succussus in coitu. ṭalle navīsī k . v. n. lit . to register the number of strokes as above. (The phrase is derived from a witty story of a banyā , who with his son and daughter-in-law were frightened into submission to their wanton will by two wags who played off a practical joke on the Bania's notorious timidity). To do nothing; to dawdle; lounge; idle away; fiddle-faddle; fritter, or fool away the time; to fold one's arms; to waste one's time.
s. m. 1. Essential nature, bottom. 2. The sole of a shoe. 3. Protection.
(from طلا) s. m. The golden border of a turband.
wety: From Sanskrit.

ety: Prk. तलओ = S. तल+कः
def: s.m. Bottom, lowest part, base; depth; keel (of a boat, &c.); sole (of a shoe, &c.); a stand, support; protection (cf. tal)
subs: (Prk. tale; S. tale), adv. & postpn., At the bottom (of), at the foot (of, -ke, but this postpn. is very commonly omitted); under, underneath, beneath, below; down; on the ground

v.n. To come down, descend, dismount, &c.; to suffer loss; to come under, be under the authority or control (of, -ke); to fall under, be trodden upon, be crushed (by); to come under (anything, for shelter), to take shelter under
adv. One over or upon another, one after another, in succession; bottom upwards, upside-down, topsy turvy; confused
tale-band bāqī, s.f. A balance sheet
(-ke), To fall under, to be crushed (by); to lie under or beneath; to lie or sleep on the ground
Great confusion to arise, great commotion or convulsion to take place.
wety: From Sanskrit.

v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune

def: s.m. Surface, level surface, level; bottom, lowest part, &c. ( = talā, q.v.); lowness, inferiority of position; low ground; the place under, underneath part; sole of the foot; palm of the hand; ground, floor; deck (of a ship); back; — adv. & postpn., Under, beneath, &c. ( = tale); from underneath, from beneath
more: n. m. S. तल surface. Low grounds. tal k , v. a. 1. To put aside or apart. 2. To hide ( chhupānā ). 3. To steal ( churānā ). tal-nazrā , n. m. One who looks from under his eyes.
s. (dakh.) See تلا talā, Bottom; beneath, below, under.
subs: (tala+atala), s.m. The fourth of the seven divisions of the infernal regions
tal-ūpar, adv. One over or upon another (as a row of water-pots, &c.); up and down; upside down, bottom upwards, topsy-turvy, in confusion
adj. & adv. Turned upside down; trodden down, ruined, destroyed (particularly crops by the treading of cattle); lost, carried off, gone
v.t. To trample down, to ruin, &c.
tal-ćuʼā (from ćūnā), s.m. Light land above clay, land that quickly becomes soft and spongy in wet weather; menstruation
(from ćhuṭnā), s.f. Sediment, grounds, lees, dregs; refuse, leavings, scum, offals
(-ke), To put underneath, to put aside or apart, to hide (with the view of carrying off or stealing)
s.m. One who looks from under his eye-lids
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

ety: S. तडाक
def: s.m. Pond, pool, tank, reservoir of water ( = talāʼo; cf. tāl).
more: n. m. S. तडाग , Pr. talāam . A tank; pond; pool. Tāl to Bhopāl tāl, aur sab talaiyāṅ haiṅ . Kabit. The only tank's Bhopal's, the rest are only ponds.
s. m. A pond, a reservoir of water, a tank. (Sans. तडाग ).

ety: inf. n. of طلب 'to seek, desire,' &c.
def: s.f. Search, quest; wish, desire; inquiry, request, demand, application, solicitation; sending for, summons; an object of quest, or of desire; — pay, wages, salary (syn. tanḵẖẉāh); — act. part. & s.m. (fr. P. t̤alab-īdan), Seeking, requiring, desiring; — seeker, desirer (used as last member of comp., e.g. ārām-t̤alab, q.v.s.v. ārām)
more: n. f. 1. ( In compos .) Seeking; seeker; requiring. 2. Wish, etc. ( chāh ). Talab lagī huī hai . 3. Solicitation ( ardās , 1). 4. Pay ( tankhwāh ). Golā bārūt kahīṅ jāë, talab se kām . Prov. Be balls and gunpowder discharged wheree'er they may, my business only is to draw my pay. talab bāṅṭnā , v. a. To distribute pay. talab bujhānā , v. a. To satisfy one's desire or appetite. Huqqā pī-kar apnī talab bujhā lo! talab-dār , n. m. 1. One addicted to drinking, smoking, etc. 2. One who has a fixed salary or wages. talab denā , v. a. To pay one his wages or salary. talab k. , v. a. 1. To call for ( bulānā , 1, 2). 2. To demand; claim. 3. To exact. talab-gār , adj. Seeking; desirous of. talab-gār , n. m. A seeker; claimant. talab-nāma , n. m. A summons ( safīnah ). ārām-talab , n. m. 1. One who is fond of ease and comfort. 2. A lazy fellow; an idler. mukarrar talab k. , v. a. 1. To call again; …

1. s. f. Pay, wages, salary. 2. Desire, wish, inquiry, search, demand, request, application, sending for, summons. 3. part. act. (of طلبيدن) in compos. Seeking, seeker, requiring. طلب کرنا t̤alab karnā, To demand, seek, require, ask for, call, summon.
subs: adj. & s.m. Asking, inquiring; — desirous; — a seeker or searcher; one who seeks the gratification of desire; one who is addicted to drinking, or smoking, &c.
or who has a fixed salary or wages
v.t. To seek; to summon, cite, call for; to require, demand, claim; to exact
adj. & s.m. Seeking, desirous (of, -kā); — seeker, searcher; desirer; claimant
(-kā), To be a seeker (of, or after), to seek, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian طَلَب (talab), from Arabic طَلَب (ṭalab).

more: (or t̤ulaba for طلبا t̤ulabā ) s. plur. (of طالب) Inquisitive, the studious, students.

Prodigality, consumption, expense. تلف المال خلف العمر talafu-l-māl ḵẖalafu-l-uﻌmr, The loss of riches is the prolongation of life. تلف کرنا talaf karnā, To ruin, to waste, consume, destroy. تلف هونا talaf honā, To be unfortunate, to meet with a loss, to be destroyed.
subs: The loss of riches is the prolongation of life

v.n. To perish, to be destroyed or ruined; to be wasted; to meet with a loss, be unfortunate.

more: v. a. See تَالنا 1. To cause to disappear or give way; to remove. Bātoṅ meṅ ṭalā dīyā . He put him off with fine words. 2. To pass in idleness; beguile one's time.

def: v.t. To cause to move or give way or flinch, to move out of the way, to remove; to put out of the way, cause to disappear, to conceal; to put off, evade; to pass in idleness, to beguile (time; — cf. ṭālnā)
more: (caus. of ٿلنا) v. a. To cause to disappear, to cause to give away.

more: s. f. A piece, a fragment.

ety: Prk. & S. तले
def: adv. & postpn. See s.v. talā. s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm ( = tāṛ, q.v.); — clapping the hands together; slapping the hands on the arms in defiance (in wrestling = tāṛī, q.v.); beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime; a musical instrument of bell-metal or brass, a sort of cymbal (played with a stick); a pair of cymbals; — the glass or pebble of an eye-glass
more: adv. Opp. of اوپر q. v. 1. Under; beneath; underneath; below; at foot. 1. Tere hī tale chār bāl * haiṅ . Double entendre. This you now see is all the crop I have. 2. Tale dhartī, ūpar Rām . Prov. The earth below and God above. (An oath). 2. Down; adown; downwards. 3. Under; next in order, degree, or rank. tale ūpar, ūpar tale , adv. 1. One upon the other ( ūpar tale ). 2. In the act; 'making the beast with two backs.' Othello. Act 1. Sc. 1. tale ūpar ke bachche , See ūpar tale ke bachche . under اوپر tale-bandī, tale band bāqī , Balance sheet. tale paṛnā , v. n. 1. To fall under; to be crushed under. 2. To lie on one's back in coitu . 3. To sleep on the ground.
subs: (˚la+ākh˚), s.f. A sort of vegetable perfume ( = tāl-parṇī)
s.m. The palm-leaf ( = tāṛ-patra, q.v.); — a hollow cylinder of gold (or a palm-leaf used as a substitute), with or without a ring attached, and thrust through the lobe of the ear as an ornament
s.f. A sort of anise, Anethum graveolens; a kind of vegetable perfume
s.m. A medicinal application to the eyes
v.n. To slap the arms defiantly (in wrestling = tāṛī ṭhoṅknā)
adj. & s.m. Having legs as long as a palm-tree; — name of a Rākshas; of a Daitya; of certain princes
v.t. To chime; to slap the arms ( = tāl ṭhoṅknā)
v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune
s.m. The medicinal herb Barleria (or Asteracantha) longifolia; the seeds of Solanum indicum (see makhānā)
s.m. Harmony, agreement, accord, &c. ( = ittifāq, q.v.); — tāl-mel khānā, v.n. To be in harmony, to agree, &c. ( = ittifāq honā)
tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.

ety: act. part. of طلع 'to rise, to appear,' &c.
def: part. adj. Rising, appearing (as the sun), arising; — s.m. Star, destiny, fate, lot, fortune; prosperity; — the (false) dawn
more: n. m. طلع rose. Lot; star ( bhāg , 3). tālٴa-shinās , n. m. A fortune-teller; soothsayer. tāle-var, tāle-mand , adj. Fortunate ( dhanī , 1, 2).

part. act. (of طلع) 1. Rising (as, the sun), arising, appearing. 2. s. m. Fortune, prosperity. 3. Destiny (good or bad), horoscope. 4. The dawn. طالع آزماٴي t̤āliﻌ-āzmā-ī, s. f. Trying oneʼs fortune. طالع شناس t̤āliﻌ-shinās, Fortuneteller, astrologer. طالع مند t̤āliﻌ-mand, adj, 1. Fortunate. 2. Wealthy. طالع مندي t̤āliﻌ-mand-ī, s. f. 1. Fortune, prosperity. 2. Wealth. طالع ور t̤āliﻌ-war, adj. 1. Fortunate. 2. Wealthy. طالع وري t̤āliﻌ-war-ī, s. f. Good fortune.
subs: s.m. An astrologer; a fortune-teller, a soothsayer
adj. Fortunate; wealthy:-t̤ālěʻ-mandī, s.f. Fortune, prosperity; wealth

ety: act. part. of طلح 'to be bad or vicious,' &c.
def: adj. Bad, corrupt, vicious, wicked, villainous.
more: (part. act. of طلح) adj. Wicked, villainous.

ety: from talā
def: adj. & s.m. Inferior, next below; — one who acts at or under the command of another.
