ety: v.n. fr. طلف
def: s.m. A gift, present.

ety: acc. of t̤alf
def: adv. For nothing, gratis.

ety: inf. n. of لفق 'to sew together'
def: s.f. Sewing together; collecting.
adj. & s.m. Furnished with cymbals; a person furnished with cymbais; an epithet of Śiva.
more: n. f. 1. (S. ताली ) A key kunjī ). 2. (S. ताल ) Clapping of the hands together. Ek hāth se tālī nahīṅ bajtī . Prov. It takes two hands to clap. (It takes two to make a quarrel). tālī bajā'nā, yā pīṭ'nā , v. a. 1. To clap one's hands (in approbation, rejoicing, or derision). Tāliyāṅ, bajā le, banno bā hogā . Nursery rhyme. Now clap your hands my baby dear, some one to wed you will come here. 2. To rejoice; to be joyful. par-tālī , n. f. A duplicate key.
n. f. S. घण्टाली 1. A small bell. 2. ( Brokers .) Eight annas.

s. f. 1. A key. 2. Clapping of the hands together. تالي ايک هاتهـ سي بجني tālī ek hāth se bajnī, is used to express an impossibility. تالي بجانا tālī bajānā, To clap the hands (generally, by way of censure and ridicule); to hoot. تالي مارنا tālī mārnā, v. a. To hoot, to damn.
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

def: s.f. A sort of instrument for opening a door; a key; — the tree Corypha taliera; a species of the mountain-palm; the plant Flacourtia cataphracta.
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: S. तालिका
def: s.f. Clapping the hands; tālī-bajānā, tālī pīṭnā, tālī mārnā, v.n. To clap the hands (in order to attract attention, or in approbation, or in delight, or in derision); to beat time by clapping the hands; to rejoice; to applaud; to deride, ridicule, to hoot
subs: lit. 'A clapping to be produced with one hand'; an impossibility to be attempted: tālī ek hāth-se (or ek hāth-se tālī) nahīṅ bajtī, prov., It takes two hands to clap, 'it takes two to make a quarrel.'
wety: From تالا (tālā, “lock”) + ـی (-ī).

ety: Prk. तलिआ = S. तल+इका
def: s.f. Bottom; sole of a shoe, &c. ( = talā, q.v.); hoof of a camel; fine or impalpable powder
more: n. f. H. tal bottom. See تلا 1. 2. Na tel talī, na ūpar palī . Prov. Nor oil at the bottom nor even in the ladle. (Said to express extreme poverty).
s. f. 1. The bottom of any thing. 2. The sole of a shoe. 3. Beneath, under. 4. Impalpable powder. ¶ تلي tillī, s. f. The spleen or milt. تپ تلي tap-tillī, s. f. Disorder of the spleen.

adv. Below, down, beneath, under. تلي اوپر tale-ūpar, adj. 1. Upside down. 2. Confused. تلي تلي ديکهنا tale tale dekhnā, To see clandestinely. تلي کي زمين اوپرهوني tale kī zamīn ūpar honī, is used to express great disturbance and confusion.
subs: s.f. A thorough cleaning or sweeping out, a clean sweep.

ety: S. ताल+इका
def: s.f. A small bell ( = ṭāl); a half-rupee piece (in brokers' cant).
more: s. f. A sort of musical instrument. See تال tāl.

def: s.m. = P طليعت t̤alīʻat, s.f. (for A. طليعة, fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.), Scout; party of scouts; a spy; vanguard; advanced post, picket; reconnoitering party; the forlorn hope (cf. t̤alāya).
more: s. m. The piquet or advanced post, the vanguard.

def: s.f. = P طليعه t̤alīʻa, s.m. (for A. طليعة, fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.), Scout; party of scouts; a spy; vanguard; advanced post, picket; reconnoitering party; the forlorn hope (cf. t̤alāya).

ety: act. part. of طلب 'to seek,' &c.
def: part. adj. & s.m. Seeking, desiring, desirous, craving, demanding, asking, interrogating; — a seeker, an inquirer, a desirer, an asker, &c.
more: n. m. طلب sought. A seeker; an inquirer. tālib-i-dīdār , Poet. n. m. Suitor; lover. tālib-i-dunyā . Worldly-minded. tālib-i-zar , adj. Fond of money; mercenary; sordid. 1. Insāf ke khuāhāṅ haiṅ, nahīṅ tālib-i-zar ham, Tahsīn-i-sakhun-fahm hai, Momin, silah apnā . Momin. Justice I seek, of riches I 've no greed, The praise of scholars, Momin, be thy meed! 2. Tālib-i-zar be-zarūr, jag meṅ khuār, Haq se dūr . The greed for wealth excessive shown, by the world despised, from God far gone. tālib-i-uqbā . One who seeks after Heaven. tālib-i-ilm, tālib-ilm, tālib-ul-ilm , n. m. lit. searcher after knowledge. A student ( biddiyārthī ). tālib-ilmī , n. f. 1. Search after knowledge; study. 2. Pupilage. 3. Apprenticeship. tālib o matlūb , Lovers ( āshiq o māshūq , 1).

part. act. (of طلب) 1. Asking, demanding, interrogating. 2. s. m. An inquirer, a seeker, a lover, asker, candidate, exacter. طالب دنيا t̤ālib-i-dunyā, Worldly-minded. طالب علم t̤ālib-i-iﻌlm, Searching after knowledge, a student. طالب علمي t̤ālib-i-iﻌlm-ī, or طالب العلمي t̤ālibu-l-iﻌlm-ī, s. f. Search of knowledge, study. (Plur. Pers. طالبان Arab. طلبا).
subs: adj. & s.m. Worldly-minded; — one who seeks worldly goods or pleasures, &c.
s.m. One who longs for the sight (of), a suitor, a lover
or t̤ālib-ě-ʻilm, or t̤ālib-ʻilm, s.m. A seeker after knowledge, a student of science or knowledge; a student
or t̤ālib-ě-ʻilmī or t̤ālib-ʻilmī, s.f. Search after knowledge, study; — the state of being a student, pupilage

ety: inf. n. ii of الف 'to cleave to,' 'to like,' &c.
def: s.f. Uniting, connecting, bringing together; uniting in friendship, reconciling; conciliating, winning; — collecting, compiling; compilation, composition; — tālīf-ě-qulūb, s.f. Uniting hearts in friendship; conciliating or winning hearts, or the art of doing so
more: n. f. الف bringing together. Compilation. tālīf-i-qulūb , n. f. Winning the hearts of the people. Sarkār Angrezī meṅ tālīf-i-qulūb nahīṅ hai . The English have not the gift of winning hearts.
(inf. ii. of الفت) s. f. 1. Connecting, uniting in friendship, reconciling. 2. Composition, compilation (of a book, &c.). تاليف کرنا tālīf karnā, To compile, compose (a book, &c.).
subs: v.t. To unite in friendship, to reconcile; to conciliate, win; — to compile, compose (a book, &c.).

def: Inferiour, next, below, one who acts at the command of another.
n. A thorough clearance or sweeping out, a clean sweep.

ety: inf. n. ii of علق 'to be suspended'
def: s.f. Hanging or suspending (a thing to a thing), suspension; delaying; — a kind of writing used by the Persians.
more: (inf. ii. of علق) s. f. 1. A kind of writing used by the Persians. 2. Suspension. 3. Delaying.

def: s.f. The palm of the hand.
s.m. Clapping the hands together ( = tālī).
s.m. corr. of تعليقه taʻlīqa, q.v. s.m. Clapping the hands together ( = tālī).
more: (corrupt. of تعليقه) s. m. An inventory, a catalogue.

ety: for A. تعليقة taʻlīqat, fem.
def: s.m. An appendix (to a book or writing); a marginal note; a schedule, an inventory, a list (of articles).
more: n. m. A schedule; inventory (of property); a list of articles.

ety: inf. n. ii of علّ; see taʻallul
def: s.f. Diverting; occupying or engaging the attention (with); changing one of the weak letters for another (in gram.); causing, occasioning; causality, causation; causing (or pronouncing, one) to have an excuse.
more: n. f. عل Diverting; altering. ( Gram .) Changing of vowels.
(inf. ii. of علت) s. f. 1. Causing one to make an excuse, occasioning. 2. Changing one of the weak letters for another (in grammar). Plur. تعليلات.

ety: inf. n. ii of علم 'to know'
def: s.f. Informing, teaching, instructing; tuition, instruction; — copying finely, writing accurately
more: n. f. علم he knew it. Imparting knowledge; teaching ( paṛhāī ); tuition; instruction; teaching (a dancing girl). tٴalīm pānā, yā lenā , v. n. To receive instruction, etc. tٴalīm denā, yā karnā , v. a. To teach; give a lesson, instruct.
(inf. ii. of علم) s. f. 1. Teaching, instruction, tuition. 2. Copying fine, writing accurately. تعليم کرنا taﻌlīm karnā, To teach.
subs: v.n. To receive instruction, to be taught, be educated
taʻlīm karnā, v.t. To teach, instruct
ex: اس باب میں انیسویں صدی کی ان کتابوں کا ذکر کیا جائے گا جو ابتدائی تعلیم کے لئے ایرانی طرز سے ہٹ کر لکھی گئیں
In this chapter, the books of the 19th century that were written contrasting to the Iranian style for primary education will be mentioned
ہمارے بچوں کی تعلیم بمقابلہ ولایتی بچوں کی تعلیم
hamāre baccoṉ kī taʻlīm bamuqābila valāyitī baccoṉ kī taʻlīm
Our children's education compared to the children education [given] abroad
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَعْلِیم (ta'līm), borrowed from Arabic تَعْلِيم (taʕlīm), from عَلَّمَ (ʕallama).

ety: see tailinī
def: s.f. A kind of beetle (found chiefly during the rainy season, the wings of which are used for blistering, as a substitute for cantharides).

s. (dakh.) A fragrant smelling plant ( Qanoon-e-islam. Perhaps, it should be تاليس پتري tālīs-patrī, and should be translated as "a tree, tālīsa, or its leaf, which is used in medicine": Wilson, Sans. Dict. ).

ety: S. तालीश+ पत्तिका
def: s.f. The plant Flacourtian cotaphracta (the leaves and young shoots of which are bitter and astringent, have the taste of rhubarb, and are considered stomachic, and given in diarrhœa, dysentery, fevers, and even in consumption).

ety: S. तालीश+ पत्तिका
def: s.f. The plant Flacourtian cotaphracta (the leaves and young shoots of which are bitter and astringent, have the taste of rhubarb, and are considered stomachic, and given in diarrhœa, dysentery, fevers, and even in consumption).
s.m. A cymbal.
s.m. = t̤alāq, q.v.

ety: the Ar. form. of تلك talk
def: s.m. Talc, mica.

ety: prob. fr. Zend thwareś: cf. tursh
def: adj. Bitter, acrid; rancid; punsent; unpalatable; acrimonious. malicious; sad, sorrowful
subs: adj. Morose, surly; of severe or stern aspect
adj. Bitter to the palate; distasteful; disappointed
v.t. To make (a thing) bitter (to or for, -par), to embitter, to aggravate; to cool or damp (joy, ardour, &c.)
adj. Of bitter speech; plain-spoken; harsh, sharp, caustic

def: s.m. Grain (or boiled rice) parched and pounded and then mixed up into paste with water; (for P. talḵẖa), the gall-bladder.
more: n. m. 1. The gall-bladder. 2. Boiled rice parched and pounded.
s. m. 1. Grain parched and pounded and then mixed up into a paste with water. 2. The gall-bladder.

def: s.f. Bitterness; pungency; acrimony, malice, rancour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تلخی (talxī). Equivalent to تلخ (talx) + ـی (-ī).

ety: inf. n. ii of لقن 'to perceive or understand'
def: s.f. Instructing, informing; instruction, religious instruction; admitting (a person) into any order in society, initiation; prayer over the dead at the time of interment, funeral service
more: (inf. ii. of لقن) s. f. Instructing, instruction, informing, religious instruction; prayer over the dead at the time of interment. تلقين کرنا talḳīn karnā, To instruct, to inform, to teach. تلقين هونا talḳīn honā, To be instructed.

ety: inf. n. of طلّ 'to rain small rain,' &c.
def: s.f. Drizzle; dew; mist.
s.m. A hillock, hill, eminence; a heap.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit तिल (tila), from Sanskrit तिल (tila), borrowed from a Munda language.

def: s.m. Impetus sive succussus in coitu; sonitus collisionis in coitu, congressus
more: s. m. Impetus sive succussus in coitu. ٿلي نويسي ṭalle-navīs-ī, s. f. Unprofitable employment, wasting of time.
subs: s.f. lit. 'Registering the number of impetuses described above'; vain, frivolous, or unprofitable employment, a wasting or frittering away of time, idling, dawdling.

def: n. m. The belly.
s. m. Congressus, sonitus collisionis in coitu.
s.f. A key.
more: The unit of length. Four tilkās make a barley corn.

def: n. m. The seeds of the Solanum Indicum ; a medicinal herb; Asteracantha longifoliā .
more: s. m. The name of a medicinal herb. (Barleria longifolia).

ety: fr. S. तड redupl., with change of t to m
def: v.n. To be restless, uneasy, or agitated, &c.; to toss about; to grieve; to be impatient; to be tantalized.
more: Mah. Wom. v. n. 1. To be agitated or uneasy; to toss about. 2. To be impatient or fidgety ( be-sabar h .). talmalāte phirnā , Mah. Wom. v. n. To walk about in restless expectation of something; to be uneasy, restless. Apne rupaë ke vāste to talmalātī phirtī hai . Wom.
v. n. 1. To be tantalized. 2. To grieve, to be agitated.
v. n. To toss about (from sickness, &c.).

ety: ṭalmal˚ = S. टल् redupl.
def: v.n. To totter; — v.t. To excite cupidity or longing in, to tantalize.
1. To totter. 2. To tantalize.
s.f. Restlessness; fidgetiness; impatience
more: n. f. Restlessness; impatience. talmalī lagnā , v. n. To be uneasy or restless.
subs: (-ko), To suffer from restlessness, to be restless or uneasy, &c.

def: v. n. (dakh.) To toss about (from sickness, &c.: Harris ).
s. m. Evasion, prevarication, putting off, shuffling, chicane.

ety: ṭāl˚ = S. तारय(ति), rt. तॄ
def: v.t. To pass over, go beyond, exceed (a fixed time); to put off, defer, postpone; to reject (a request); to elude by subterfuge, to evade, prevaricate; to avoid; to put or turn aside, to put or turn (one) out of the way, to deter, frighten away; to remove, avert, ward off, fend, obviate, prevent
more: (trans. of ٿلنا) v. a. 1. To evade, prevaricate, to avoid, put off, postpone, defer. 2. To drive out of the way, put or turn aside, ward off, fend, obviate, move, remove, prevent.
wety: Urdu verb set ٹَلْنا (ṭalnā) ٹالْنا (ṭālnā) Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀝𑀸𑀮𑀤𑀺 (ṭāladi, “removes, frightens away”), from Sanskrit टालयति (ṭālayati, “cause to move”).

ety: ṭal˚ = S. तर(ति), rt. तॄ; cf. ṭālnā
def: v.n. To move, stir; to be displaced, be dislocated (as a bone, &c.); to give way, to shrink, flinch; to retire, make off, get out of the way, sheer off, decamp, disappear, vanish; to pass off, pass over, pass by or away (as a fixed time or season for anything, or a danger or evil); to be evaded or shirked; to fail of observance, to withdraw, draw back or out (of a promise or agreement, &c.)
more: v. n. 1. To give way, shrink from, flinch. 2. To disappear, pass away, retire.

ety: see talan
def: v.t. To fry; — v.n. To be fried (in butter or oil).
more: v. a. To fry or be fried in butter or oil.

v. n. 1. To move; stir; take oneself off; disappear; vanish. 1. Kāl ṭale, par katāl na ṭale . Prov. Death may be put off, but the bottle never. (The drunkard makes death wait till he has obeyed the summons from the ale-house). 2. Kal bhī mahfil se terī ham na ṭale, baiṭh gaë, Bole 'uṭh, uṭh' sabhī, yāṅtak ki gale baiṭh gaë . The persistent lover. Even yesterday from your assembly I would not turn out, I stuck all through though all were hoarse with crying out, 'out, out'! 3. Khāë ke beryāṅ jamelisi, kām ke beryāṅ ṭari jālisi . Bhoj. At meal time she all ready sits, at working time away she flits. 4. Ṭālā nahīṅ ṭaltā . The inevitable will not be put off. 5. Āj maiṅ bin horī khele to se, nahīṅ jāūṅgī, Banvārī, jo ho so ho; Sāṅchi jānyo kahat hūṅ to se, lākhan bāt banā mat mo se, Ṭarūṅgī na tere ṭāre, jo ho so ho! Holī. Mauj. To-day, Kanhaīyā, I'll not budge, with you I'll Holī play, Come t…

def: v. n. 1. To postpone; defer. 2. To prevaricate ( ālā bālā batānā ). 2. Hazār dam haiṅ use yād, tū ne dekhā, Zauq, Gayā woh gair ke ghar tujh ko ṭāl-ke kaisā! Zauq. 3. To drive out of the way; put or turn aside; remove. 4. To prevent; obviate; avert; ward off; fend.

v.t. To chime; to slap the arms ( = tāl ṭhoṅknā)
v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune
s.m. The medicinal herb Barleria (or Asteracantha) longifolia; the seeds of Solanum indicum (see makhānā)
s.m. Harmony, agreement, accord, &c. ( = ittifāq, q.v.); — tāl-mel khānā, v.n. To be in harmony, to agree, &c. ( = ittifāq honā)
tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.

def: adj. See s.v. tal. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).
+ Wom. adj. 1. Turned upside down. 2. Destroyed; ruined, more especially crops by the trampling of the cattle. 3. Gone; lost; stolen.
To destroy.
s.m. Slapping the hands together.
v.n. To shake; to crackle, &c. = taṛtaṛānā, q.v. v.n. To be drenched or soaked, to drip with moisture or grease.
more: v. n. To be loose (the bowels).

def: s. m. Evasion, prevarication, putting off, shuffling, chicane.

ety: S. तालुकः
def: s.m. The palate, the ridge or slit in the roof of the mouth; — the membraneous part found in new-born infants at the coronal and sagittal commissures; — a disorder in horses, the lampreys
more: n. m. S. तालु 1. The palate, the ridge or slit in the roof of the mouth. 2. A disorder in horses; the lampreys. tālū-anchhar , n. m. S. तालव्य The palatals. tālū se zabān na lagnā , v. n. To talk without ceasing; run (one's tongue) like scissors. Zabān tālū se nahīṅ lagtī . The tongue wont stick to the palate. (Said of one who talks without ceasing).
s. m. 1. The palate, the ridge or slit in the roof of the mouth. 2. A disorder in horses, the lampreys.
s. (dakh.) Crown of the head. تالوپو tālū po, Over the head.
subs: (-kā), To raise the uvula (of a new-born infant)

ety: S. तल+कः, with व inserted
def: s.m. The sole of the foot; the heel
more: n. m. S. तल bottom. The sole of the foot. 1. Talve bhulas gaë . 2. Zabān kī sī kahūṅ, yā talve kī sī? Prov. Shall I speak as my tongue dictates (truth), or as my heel (from interested motives). talvā khujlānā, yā khujanā , v. n. lit . To itch (the sole of the foot). It is considered a sign of an impending journey. talvoṅ se lagnā , v. n. lit . To touch the sole of the foot. To affect or touch one; to appeal to one's interests or feelings; to hang on to one; to pursue closely. Talvoṅ se lagī, sir meṅ ko nikal gaī . Wom. It struck me in the heel, and came out at my head. (I am burning with rage from head to heel.) talvoṅ se malnā , v. n. To crush; trample. talve chāṭnā, yā sahlānā , v. n. To flatter; coax; wheedle.
n. m. A small knotted piece of wood.
W. n. m. The owner of a ṭāl , q. v. Ṭalvā ihāṅ se ek rupaiyā ke lakrī jokhā lavā, jarnā taniko nai khe . Mag. Get a rupee's worth of wood from the woodman's. There is no fire-wood in the house.

s. m. The sole of the foot. تلواچاٿنا talūʼā chāṭnā, or تلوي تلي هاتهـ دهرنا talwe tale hāth dharnā, To flatter. تلوي تلاسي talwe-talāsī, s. f. (dakh.) Rubbing of the soles of the feet. تلوي سـﮩ‍لاني talwe sahlāne (lit. To stroke the soles of the feet) signifies excessive flattery, adulation. تلوون سي آگ لگني talwoṅ se āg lagnī (lit. burning in the soles of the feet) implies excessive anger.
subs: v.n. To feel the soles burning'; to be on fire, be excessively angry
(-ke), 'To touch or stick to the soles of the feet'; to touch or affect (one), to touch the feelings or interests (of); to follow close at the heels (of), dog the steps (of), to pursue closely
(-ke), 'To place the hand under the soles (of)'; to flatter, &c. = next, q.v.
(-ke), 'To lick the soles of the feet (of)'; to flatter, coax, wheedle
(-ke), 'To stroke the soles of the feet (of)'; to flatter, &c. = talwe ćāṭnā.

ety: ṭāl, q.v.+S. कः
def: s.m. The man who watches over a ṭāl or store of wood, &c.; the owner of a ṭāl.
more: s. m. A man who watches over a ٿال ṭāl, q. v.

def: adj. 1. Upside down. 2. Confused.
s.m. The palate ( = tālū).

ety: or طالوط t̤ālūt̤
def: s.m. Saul (the king).
n. m. A present of sugar, fruits, etc. made to a lying-in-woman.
v. n. (dakh.) To have the feet blistered from the heat of the sun.

ety: see talwāsnā
def: s.m. Abrasion, or blister, on the soles of the feet.
more: n. m. A blister on the sole of the foot.

ety: S. तरवारिः
def: s.f. A sword ( = tarwār)
more: n. f. S. तरवारी A sword; scimitar; sabre; broadsword. talvār chalānā yā mārnā, talvār se uṛānā , v. a. To cut at one with a sword; use a sword. talvār chalanā , v. n. To fight with swords. talvār sūṅtnā, yā kheṅchnā , v. n. To draw or unsheathe the sword. talvār miyān k . v. a. To sheathe the sword. talvār kā ghāo , n. m. A sword cut. talvār kī āṅch , n. f. The sword or dagger's flash. talvāroṅ kī chhāṅ meṅ , adv. lit . Under the shadow of swords; hence, well-guarded.
subs: v.n. Sword to resound; to be busy with (one's) sword
v.n. To brandish a sword; to use a sword; to strike with the sword
v.n. Swords to be busy, to be fighting with swords
talwār khaiṅćnā, v.n. To unsheathe or draw the sword
s.f. The flash of a sword
v.t. To strike or cut with a sword, to use a sword
adv. 'Under the shadow of swords'; well-guarded.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi تلْوار (tlvar /⁠talvār⁠/) (c. 1606) / تروار (trvar /⁠tarvār⁠/) (c. 1611), from Sanskrit तरवारि (taravāri, “sword; scimitar”).
